Major Producers (12)

Ranked by latest disclosed Mn ore production

Companies ranked by most recently disclosed annual manganese ore production (kilotonnes). Each card links to the primary source (annual report, production report, or exchange filing). "Not disclosed" means the company does not publish metal-specific tonnage — common for private Chinese/state-owned groups and pre-production projects.

#1Comilog / Société Minière de la Moanda (Gabon — Eramet subsidiary)
France
6,803 kt ore FY2024
Eramet FY2024 Results
#2Eramet S.A. — Comilog (Moanda Mine, Gabon)
France
6,803 kt ore FY2024

South Africa
3,761 kt ore FY2025

#4Tshipi é Ntle Manganese Mining (Pty) Ltd. — Tshipi Borwa Mine
South Africa
3,603 kt ore FY2025

Australia
2,151 kt ore FY2025

#6MOIL Limited (Manganese Ore India Limited)
India
1,756 kt ore FY2024
Annual Report 2023-24
#7Consolidated Minerals (Consmin) — Woodie Woodie Mine
Australia
Not disclosed Undisclosed
Key mine: Woodie Woodie (high-grade ~40% Mn ore).
ConsMin website
#8Ghana Manganese Company Ltd. (GMC)
Ghana
Not disclosed
Nsuta mines; 26.9% Mn (2019 sample); no FY24/25 ore production disclosed on company site.
GMC Mine Overview
#9Grupo Industrial Camargo Corrêa / Mineração Pedra Branca do Amapari (Brazil)
Brazil
Not disclosed
Undisclosed; primarily gold/iron operations at Pedra Branca do Amapari, no primary Mn source found
Construtora Camargo Corrêa
#10Guyana Manganese Inc. (wholly owned subsidiary of Bosai Group, China)
China
Not disclosed
Matthews Ridge mine; 38% Mn per USGS citing company
Bosai Minerals
#11JSC Nikopol Ferroalloy Plant (Ferrexpo / Privat Group related — Ukraine)
Ukraine
Not disclosed FY2024
Ferroalloy producer using manganese sinter/agglomerate; no ore mining output disclosed in primary financial/audit reports.
Financial Reports Page

Australia
